events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl, a well-known writer on music and musical subjects, was born at Iserlohn in Westphalia on December 5, 1831.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: NOHL, Carl Friedrich Ludwig, a well-known writer on music and musical subjects, was born at Iserlohn in Westphalia, on Dec. 5, 1831.

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl entered the University of Bonn in 1850.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: He was educated at the Gymnasium of Duisburg, and in 1850 entered the University of Bonn.

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl entered the Prussian Civil Service as Referendarius in 1853.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: In 1853 Nohl entered the Prussian Civil Service as Referendarius, but in 1856 his health broke down, and he had to undertake a journey to France and Italy.

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl returned to Berlin in 1857 and continued his musical studies under Professor Kiel.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: He returned to Berlin in 1857, and continued his musical studies under Professor Kiel.

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl finally abandoned the legal profession and settled at Heidelberg in 1858.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: In 1858 he finally abandoned the legal profession, and settled at Heidelberg, the University of which place conferred upon him the degree of Doctor of Philosophy (1860).

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl received the degree of Doctor of Philosophy from the University of Heidelberg in 1860.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: In 1858 he finally abandoned the legal profession, and settled at Heidelberg, the University of which place conferred upon him the degree of Doctor of Philosophy (1860).

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl went to Munich in 1861, where in 1865 King Ludwig II appointed him an Honorary Professor in the University.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: In the following year he went to Munich, where in 1865 King Ludwig II. appointed him an Honorary Professor in the University.

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl returned to Heidelberg in 1872, where he resided as teacher of musical history and aesthetics until his death on December 16, 1885.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: In 1872 he returned to Heidelberg, where he resided as teacher of musical history and festhetics until his death, Dec. 16, 1885.

events.summary: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl died in Heidelberg on December 16, 1885.

people.name: Carl Friedrich Ludwig Nohl
people.description: German writer on music and musical subjects

events.excerpt: In 1872 he returned to Heidelberg, where he resided as teacher of musical history and festhetics until his death, Dec. 16, 1885.

events.summary: Domenico da Nola was born in Nola in the kingdom of Naples in the first quarter of the 16th century.

place.name: Nola
place.description: City in the kingdom of Naples, Italy

events.excerpt: NOLA, Domenico DA, whose proper name seems to have been Don Joan Domenico del Giovane, was born at Nola in the kingdom of Naples in the first quarter of the 16th century.

events.summary: Domenico da Nola served as Maestro di Cappella of the Church of the Annunziata in Naples.
events.excerpt: He was afterwards Maestro di Cappella of the Church of the Annunziata, Naples.

events.summary: Claudio Merulo re-edited the Villanelle of Domenico da Nola in 1567.
events.excerpt: His Villanelle were afterwards re-edited by Claudio Merulo in 1567.

events.summary: Gounod's opera La Nonne Sanglante, with words by Scribe and Delavigne, was produced at the Paris Grand Opéra on October 18, 1854.
events.excerpt: NONNE SANGLANTE, LA. Opera in five acts; words by Scribe and Delavigne, from Lewis's 'Monk'; music by Gounod. Produced at the Paris Grand Opéra, Oct. 18, 1854.

events.summary: Anthony van Noordt was organist of the Nieuwe Kerk at Amsterdam in 1659, when he published a Tabulatur-Boeck for the organ.
events.excerpt: NOORDT or NOORTH, Anthony van, is only known as being in 1659 organist of the Nieuwe Kerk at Amsterdam. In that year there was published of him a Tabulatur-Boeck for the organ containing a number of the French Psalm tunes varied according to the verses, and six Fantasias in Fugue-form.

events.summary: Daniel Norcome, a lay-clerk of St. George's Chapel, Windsor, was born there in 1576.

place.name: Windsor
place.description: Town in England, home of St. George's Chapel

events.excerpt: NORCOME, Daniel, son of a lay-clerk of St. George's Chapel, Windsor, and himself a lay-clerk, was born there in 1576.

events.summary: Daniel Norcome contributed a madrigal, 'With angel's face and brightness,' to 'The Triumphs of Oriana' in 1601.
events.excerpt: He contributed a madrigal, 'With angel's face and brightness,' to 'The Triumphs of Oriana,' 1601.

events.summary: Lillian Nordica, born Lillian Norton, was born on May 12, 1859, at Farmington, Maine, United States.
events.excerpt: NORDICA, Lilian, or Lillian, nie Norton, was born May 12, 1859, at Farmington, Maine, U.S.A.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica sang at the Crystal Palace with Gilmore's American band on May 21 and 22, 1878.
events.excerpt: On May 21 and 22, 1878, she came to England and sang at the Crystal Palace with Gilmore's American band.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica made her successful debut under the name of Nordica at Brescia on April 30, 1879, as Violetta in 'Traviata.'
events.excerpt: on April 30, 1879, made a successful début, under the name of Nordica, at Brescia as Violetta in 'Traviata.'

events.summary: Lilian Nordica was engaged in 1881 at St. Petersburg and sang as Philine and Amalia in 'Un Ballo.'
events.excerpt: she was engaged in 1881 at St. Petersburg, and sang as Philine, Amalia in 'Un Ballo,' etc.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica made her debut in 'Faust' at St. Petersburg on July 21, 1882.
events.excerpt: On July 21, 1882, she made her début in 'Faust' and sang on Dec. 15 in 'Hamlet,'

events.summary: Lilian Nordica sang in 'Hamlet' at St. Petersburg on December 15, 1882.
events.excerpt: On July 21, 1882, she made her début in 'Faust' and sang on Dec. 15 in 'Hamlet,'

events.summary: Lilian Nordica reappeared on the stage at Boston in 1885.
events.excerpt: In 1885 she reappeared on the stage at Boston, made a long tour with Mapleson in America and the English provinces,

events.summary: Lilian Nordica made her debut at Covent Garden under Mapleson on March 12, 1887, as Violetta.
events.excerpt: under him on March 12, 1887, she made her début at Covent Garden as Violetta—appearing later as Gilda and Margaret with instant success,

events.summary: Lilian Nordica sang at the Philharmonic on March 24, 1887.
events.excerpt: On March 24 she sang at the Philharmonic.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica created the part of Zelioa in Stanford's 'Veiled Prophet' on July 26, 1893.
events.excerpt: On July 26, 1893, she created the part of Zelioa in Stanford's 'Veiled Prophet.'

events.summary: Lilian Nordica sang Elsa in 'Lohengrin' at Bayreuth in 1894.
events.excerpt: In 1894 she sang Elsa in 'Lohengrin' at Bayreuth.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica sang again at the Handel Festival in 1897.
events.excerpt: In 1897 she sang again at the Handel Festival.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica returned to Covent Garden in 1898.
events.excerpt: In 1898 and 1899 and again in 1902 she returned to Covent Garden, and added to her repertory Donna Anna, Susanna, Isolde, Briinnhilde, etc.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica returned to Covent Garden in 1899.
events.excerpt: In 1898 and 1899 and again in 1902 she returned to Covent Garden, and added to her repertory Donna Anna, Susanna, Isolde, Briinnhilde, etc.

events.summary: Lilian Nordica returned to Covent Garden in 1902.
events.excerpt: In 1898 and 1899 and again in 1902 she returned to Covent Garden, and added to her repertory Donna Anna, Susanna, Isolde, Briinnhilde, etc.

events.summary: F. Corder's opera Nordisa, with words and music by the composer, was produced by the Carl Rosa Company at Liverpool on January 26, 1887.
events.excerpt: NORDISA. Romantic opera in three acts, words and music by F. Corder. Produced by the Carl Rosa Company at Liverpool on Jan. 26, and at Drury Lane, May 4, 1887.

events.summary: F. Corder's opera Nordisa was produced at Drury Lane on May 4, 1887.
events.excerpt: Produced by the Carl Rosa Company at Liverpool on Jan. 26, and at Drury Lane, May 4, 1887.

events.summary: Richard Nordraak was born at Christiania on June 12, 1842.

place.name: Christiania
place.description: Capital of Norway, formerly known as Christiania

events.excerpt: He was born at Christiania, June 12, 1842, and was a pupil of Kiel and Kullak

events.summary: Richard Nordraak died on March 20, 1866.
events.excerpt: his early death on March 20, 1866, deprived his friend and his country of an enthusiastic supporter.

events.summary: Bellini's opera Norma, with words by Romani, was produced at Milan on December 26, 1831.
events.excerpt: NORMA. Opera in two acts; words by Romani, music by Bellini. Produced at Milan, Dec. 26, 1831

events.summary: Bellini's opera Norma was produced at the Paris Italiens theatre on December 8, 1835.
events.excerpt: In Paris, Italiens, Dec. 8, 1835.

events.summary: Bellini's opera Norma was produced at the King's Theatre in London on June 20, 1833, in Italian.
events.excerpt: In London, in Italian, King's Theatre, June 20, 1833

events.summary: Bellini's opera Norma was produced at Drury Lane in English on June 24, 1837.
events.excerpt: in English (Planché), Drury Lane, June 24, 1837.

events.summary: Barak Norman, an English viol and violoncello maker, went into partnership with Nathaniel Cross at London in 1715.
events.excerpt: In 1715 Barak Norman went into partnership with Nathaniel Cross and carried on a joint business with him at the sign of the Bass Viol in St. Paul's Churchyard.

events.summary: Thomas Norris, Mus.B., was born at Mere near Salisbury in August 1741.
events.excerpt: NORRIS, Thomas, Mus.B., born at Mere near Salisbury, in August 1741 (baptized on the 15th),

events.summary: Thomas Norris appeared as one of the principal soprani at the Worcester Festival in 1761.
events.excerpt: He appeared as one of the principal soprani at the Worcester Festival, 1761,

events.summary: Thomas Norris appeared at the Hereford Festival in 1762.
events.excerpt: and Hereford Festival, 1762,

events.summary: Thomas Norris appeared at Drury Lane in 'The Spring,' a pasticcio, in 1762.
events.excerpt: and in the latter year at Drury Lane in 'The Spring,' a pasticcio.

events.summary: Thomas Norris was appointed organist of Christ Church Cathedral in Oxford in 1765.
events.excerpt: In 1765 he was appointed organist of Christ Church Cathedral, Oxford

events.summary: Thomas Norris graduated at Oxford as Mus.B. in November 1765, with his exercise performed on November 12.
events.excerpt: in November of the same year graduated at Oxford as Mus.B., his exercise (two anthems, 'The Lord is king' and 'I will alway give thanks') being performed in the Music School, Nov. 12

events.summary: Thomas Norris was chosen organist of St. John's College, Oxford on December 15, 1765.
events.excerpt: and on Dec. 15 was chosen organist of St. John's College.

events.summary: Thomas Norris appeared at the Gloucester Festival in 1766 as a tenor singer.
events.excerpt: In 1766 he appeared at the Gloucester Festival as a tenor singer,

events.summary: Thomas Norris was admitted a lay clerk of Magdalen College, Oxford on November 5, 1771.
events.excerpt: On Nov. 5, 1771, he was admitted a lay clerk of Magdalen College, Oxford,

events.summary: Thomas Norris sang at the Commemoration of Handel in 1784, where his delivery of the final recitatives in 'Israel in Egypt' and of 'Thy rebuke' and 'Behold and see' in 'Messiah' was greatly admired.
events.excerpt: He sang at the Commemoration of Handel in 1784 (where his delivery of the final recitatives in 'Israel in Egypt,' and of 'Thy rebuke,' and 'Behold and see,' in 'Messiah,' was greatly admired),

events.summary: Thomas Norris was engaged at the Birmingham Festival in 1790.
events.excerpt: In 1790 he was engaged at the Birmingham Festival.

events.summary: Thomas Norris died at Himley Hall, near Stourbridge, on September 3, 1790.
events.excerpt: ten days afterwards (Sept. 3, 1790), he expired at Himley Hall, near Stourbridge,
